stories.title: DCS hook frame parser + ANSI streaming (post Amendment 5)
stories.verifiedBy: codex round-3 PASS @ warp-src b65ccee + main 6a68c41 (3 rounds: AC#7 amend + truecolor + unknown DCS + cap overflow + empty 7-bit ST)
acceptanceCriteria: Per Plan Amendment 5: DCS schema already extracted into facade::app_terminal::ansi::dcs_hooks (M3-S03 v2 commit c76f876). M3-S05 adds the streaming frame detector + hex decoder + dispatch.

Implement streaming DCS detection in TerminalModel::ingest_pty_bytes: state machine recognizes ESC P dollar d <hex_chars> ST (where ST = 0x9c or ESC backslash); accumulates hex bytes; on ST: hex-decode body and call DProtoHook::deserialize. **Non-Warp DCS sequences (any other markers) MUST use an ignore-until-ST state — do NOT route through DcsRaw/finish_dcs which can increment parse errors and dcs_hook_count counters.**

Wire DProtoHook events: Precmd marks new pending block start (deferred to S07); Preexec records command text/start_time (deferred to S07); CommandFinished sets exit_code + finalizes Block (deferred to S07). For S05 baseline: just log/emit DProtoHook events; full Block aggregation in S07.

Add minimal ANSI streaming parser for plain-text + cursor-positioning + SGR color attributes. Specifically support: CSI n m (SGR), CSI n;m H (cursor pos), CSI K (erase line), newline carriage-return backspace tab. Ignore unknown CSI sequences gracefully (do not print). **For SGR truecolor/256-color: when seeing 38;2;R;G;B or 48;2;R;G;B (24-bit) or 38;5;N or 48;5;N (256-color), MUST skip the operand triplet/index — do NOT parse R/G/B/N as independent SGR codes (which would incorrectly apply 2 as dim, 0 as reset, etc).**

Renderer integration choice: keep Option A static_grid baseline OR flip to Option B dynamic_grid.rs. Worker decides based on per-cell distinct rendering needs. If Option B: new file warp-src/crates/warpui/src/platform/android/dynamic_grid.rs that supports per-cell color/glyph from terminal model cells.

Tests: at least 3 new tests feeding actual zsh_body.sh hex JSON DCS samples (lines 80, 254, 301 from warp-src/app/assets/bundled/bootstrap/zsh_body.sh) through the parser; verify DProtoHook Precmd / Preexec / CommandFinished are correctly emitted

**AC#7 (AMENDED 2026-04-30 post codex round-1)**: Verification: cargo ndk check -p warp_terminal_mobile_facade + warpui passes; cargo test passes (at least 17 tests total); device test on S24 Ultra R5CX10VFFBA passes the parser/device-log gate (sgr_apply_count >=4 + dcs_hook_count >=2 + dcs_error_count == 0 + per-event sgr_color logs showing fg=0xCC0000FF for code 31, fg=0x4E9A06FF for code 32, fg=0x3465A4FF for code 34, fg=0xFFFFFFFF for reset). **On-screen per-cell colored rendering is DEFERRED to M3-S08 (the per-cell renderer story) — that story gates final visual rendering acceptance.** This amendment is justified by the parser/render scope split: M3-S05 owns the intelligence layer (correctness via parser tests + device logcat); M3-S08 owns the rendering layer (visual via screenshot + ls -la /system).
Codex review dispatched and PASS verdict received

Asset packaging: copy warp-src/app/assets/bundled/bootstrap/zsh_body.sh to android/app/src/main/assets/warp/zsh_body.sh via Gradle copy task (similar pattern to M2-S02 jniLibs Gradle copy). APK packaging includes the asset.

Runtime accessibility: APK asset readable from PTY context via AssetManager.open("warp/zsh_body.sh") + write to /data/data/dev.warp.mobile/files/warp/zsh_body.sh on first launch (or AssetFileDescriptor pattern). Add JNI export Java_dev_warp_mobile_NativeBridge_extractWarpAssets if needed.

Hook execution constraint (DEFERRED to M5 Termux Runtime): S24 Ultra has only /system/bin/sh (mksh) — no zsh available. zsh_body.sh requires zsh's precmd/preexec mechanism. M3-S06 ships the asset and verifies it's loadable but does NOT verify hook execution end-to-end. Hook execution gate moves to M5 when Termux brings zsh.

Web search docs cited (Android AssetManager API; Termux package availability for zsh; AGPL asset bundling compliance).

stories.title: Block model extract + DCS event aggregation (Plan Amendment 5; M3 Acceptance #3)
stories.verifiedBy: codex round-1 PASS @ warp-src d943f1c + main 2d73979 (M3 Acceptance #3 met on S24U)
acceptanceCriteria: Per Plan Amendment 5: extract app::terminal::model::block + blocks into facade::app_terminal::model::block + blocks (continuing M3-S03-v2 extraction pattern). Block struct fields: start_time / command / exit_code / output_range — verify upstream app::terminal::model::Block struct shape.

DCS parser from M3-S05 emits BlockEvent on hook delivery: Precmd → BlockEvent::Start(start_time, command_placeholder); Preexec → fills in command; CommandFinished → BlockEvent::End(exit_code) — wire through TerminalModel::ingest_pty_bytes after DProtoHook::deserialize.

Block aggregation in TerminalModel: maintain Vec<Block>; on Start emit new block; on End update current block exit_code + finalize. Test via synthetic DCS injection (TerminalSimulationReceiver TERM_INJECT_RAW from M3-S05) since S24U has no zsh.
JNI debug accessor: Java_dev_warp_mobile_NativeBridge_terminalBlocksDump returns JSON array of {start_time, command, exit_code} for current Vec<Block> state.

Hook execution constraint (DEFERRED to M5 Termux per M3-S06 amendment): real zsh hooks via PTY require zsh which is M5. M3-S07 verification uses synthetic DCS injection per the M3-S05 path; AC#4 zsh sample run moves to M5.

Verification (M3 Acceptance #3): inject 3 synthetic DCS sequences mirroring zsh ls/whoami/false flow (Precmd + Preexec + CommandFinished triplets, exit codes 0/0/1) via TerminalSimulationReceiver; verify 3 Block entries via terminalBlocksDump + each has correct command + exit_code.
Driver: tools/scripts/test-block-model.sh <serial> drives synthetic injection + verification; result.json with block_count, command_match[], exit_code_match[], gate.overall_pass.

stories.title: Per-cell dynamic_grid renderer + Live ls -la /system on S24U (M3 Acceptance #1, post-amend)
stories.verifiedBy: codex round-1 PASS @ warp-src 6dedd95 + main 83c548b
acceptanceCriteria: Build warp-src/crates/warpui/src/platform/android/dynamic_grid.rs (NEW): per-cell renderer that reads from TerminalModel.cells() with SGR fg/bg/attrs honored. Reuses M2-S07 glyph atlas + cosmic-text shaping. The static_grid M2-S08 path stays untouched (M2 acceptance held).
Window::push_frame routing: prefer dynamic_grid when TerminalModel has non-default cells (i.e. PTY-driven content); fall back to static_grid for the M2-S08 demo flow when terminal_mode flag is false.

Per-cell color verification (synthetic SGR injection): inject \x1b[31mRED\x1b[32mGREEN\x1b[34mBLUE\x1b[0m via TerminalSimulationReceiver TERM_INJECT_RAW; capture screenshot; visual inspection shows distinct red/green/blue text in their respective cells (NOT the M3-S05 static_grid replication artifact).
Line wrap on real PTY: spawn /system/bin/sh; run `/system/bin/ls -la /system\n`; output wraps at 80-col boundary; ≥40 lines visible on screen (or scrollable; scrollback test is M3-S09).

Toybox ls color limitation DEFERRED to M5: Android's stock toybox /system/bin/ls does NOT emit ANSI colors even with --color=always. M3-S08 verifies per-cell color rendering via synthetic SGR injection (above); M5 Termux brings GNU coreutils ls --color=auto for full real-PTY colored verification.

Linux baseline pixel similarity gate DEFERRED to M5: requires a Linux reference render with matching font/cell-size which is out of scope for M3-S08. M3-S08 verifies functional correctness (per-cell colors visible + line-wrap + ≥40 ls lines rendered) instead of pixel-equality.

Driver: tools/scripts/test-dynamic-grid.sh R5CX10VFFBA — install APK, launch terminal_mode, inject RED/GREEN/BLUE synthetic SGR, capture screenshot.png; spawn ls -la /system, capture screenshot-ls.png; emit result.json with sgr_color_test_pass, ls_lines_visible, gate.overall_pass.
Result artifact: .omc/m3-artifacts/M3-S08-color-test.png (synthetic SGR), M3-S08-ls-output.png (real ls), M3-S08-result.json (gate evidence).
Codex review dispatched and PASS verdict received

stories.title: First-launch atomic extraction (JNI shim with usr.tmp → usr rename + kill-mid-extract recovery)
stories.verifiedBy: codex round-3 PASS @ main ab8d206. Round-1 found 3 findings (BLOCKING symlink path-confinement; MEDIUM first-launch UI; HIGH concurrency single-flight). Round-2 fixed all 3 but introduced status-0 contract bug (AtomicBool fire-and-forget on contention returned Ok before bootstrap actually ready). Round-3 replaced AtomicBool with Mutex<()> — concurrent callers block until in-flight install completes, then re-enter and sha-pin short-circuits. Status 0 = bootstrap genuinely ready. Device verified on S24U R5CX10VFFBA: clean first launch 5358 ms; sha-pin fast path 1 ms; kill-mid-extract recovery 6304 ms; LD_LIBRARY_PATH-unset zsh+git+bash launch (M4-S03 patchelf RUNPATH end-to-end). Implementation in crates/android-host/src/bootstrap.rs (NOT bootstrap_install.c — language deviation per codex round-1 non-blocking note; AC#1 amended). 15 host tests pass.
acceptanceCriteria: crates/android-host/src/bootstrap.rs (NEW) implements: extract bootstrap-aarch64.zip from APK assets to /data/data/dev.warp.mobile/files/usr.tmp/; verify SHA256; atomic rename usr.tmp/ → usr/; write version-pin (deviation from C scaffold per codex round-1 non-blocking: Rust + JNI in existing android-host cdylib, consistent with M1+ pattern; JNI export name is the contract)
Kill-mid-extract recovery: on next launch, detect usr.tmp/ remnant and restart extraction (not skip with corrupt usr/)
JNI export Java_dev_warp_mobile_NativeBridge_bootstrapInstall returns extraction status (0=success, !=0=error code)
Subsequent launches: verify extraction by checking version.json sha256 match; instant return without re-extraction

Verification: install APK on S24 Ultra R5CX10VFFBA; first launch shows bootstrap install progress; subsequent launch instant (no progress dialog); kill-mid-extract test (am force-stop during install) recovers cleanly on next launch
Codex review dispatched and PASS verdict received

ELF DT_RUNPATH retargeting verification: $PREFIX/bin/zsh and $PREFIX/bin/git launch on S24 Ultra with LD_LIBRARY_PATH explicitly UNSET (proves RUNPATH alone suffices). Verify via `adb shell run-as dev.warp.mobile env -u LD_LIBRARY_PATH /data/data/dev.warp.mobile/files/usr/bin/zsh -c 'echo $0'` returns `zsh` exit 0; same with `env -u LD_LIBRARY_PATH $PREFIX/bin/git --version` returns 2.x exit 0 (M4-S03 round-4 carry-forward: build-bootstrap.sh patchelf step ensures RUNPATH is correct in zip; M4-S05 verifies post-extraction that dynamic linking succeeds without env crutch)

Round-2 fixed all 4 + introduced recursion bug (.zshenv-zsh_body.sh infinite loop). Round-3 added WARP_ZSH_BODY_SOURCING sentinel (visible to same-process re-entry, unset post-source). Device verified S24U: hooks emit exactly 1 CommandFinished + 1 Precmd + 1 Bootstrapped frame per zsh -c (M3-S05 DCS parser consumable); git --exec-path returns dev.warp.mobile path. M3-S06 hook execution deferral CLOSED end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: WarpTerminalService.spawnPty default program changed from /system/bin/sh to /data/data/dev.warp.mobile/files/usr/bin/zsh
Environment: PATH=$PREFIX/bin:$PATH, HOME=$PREFIX/home, ZDOTDIR=$PREFIX/etc (where zsh_body.sh ships per M3-S06)
zsh_body.sh hooks fire correctly via precmd/preexec — verifies M3-S06 deferred path
Device verification: spawn shell on S24U R5CX10VFFBA; whoami returns u0_aXXXX (app UID); echo $0 returns zsh; ls --color=auto produces ANSI escapes (closes M3-S08 AC#5 toybox-color deferral)
Codex review dispatched and PASS verdict received

zsh runtime-config override (M4-S03 round-6+7+8 carry-forward): zsh 5.9 IGNORES the inherited MODULE_PATH env var (reinitializes `module_path` from compile-time default). FPATH IS honored as env var (imported into `fpath`) — but the compile-time default ALSO includes stale `/data/data/com.termux/files/usr/share/zsh/{5.9/functions,site-functions}` entries that survive into `$fpath` until explicitly filtered. Canonical fix in $ZDOTDIR/.zshenv: `module_path=($PREFIX/lib/zsh/5.9)` (replace, not append) AND `fpath=($PREFIX/share/zsh/5.9/functions $PREFIX/share/zsh/site-functions ${fpath:#/data/data/com.termux/*})` (round-8 fix: append filter `${fpath:#/data/data/com.termux/*}` removes stale compile-time entries that shell-array preservation would otherwise carry forward). HOME and ZDOTDIR DO work as env vars at spawn. Device verification (round-8 corrected): (a) `print -rl -- $module_path` returns ONLY `/data/data/dev.warp.mobile/files/usr/lib/zsh/5.9` AND `print -rl -- $fpath | grep -F com.termux | wc -l` returns `0` (proves zero stale entries after filter), (b) `print -rl -- $fpath | grep -F /files/usr/share/zsh | wc -l` returns ≥2 (functions + site-functions present at the dev-rooted path; round-8 fix: grep the `/files/usr/share/zsh` portion that's stable across the prefix renames), (c) `zmodload zsh/zutil && echo loaded` returns `loaded` (proves module_path loads modules), (d) `autoload -Uz compinit && compinit -D -i && whence -w compinit _git` returns `compinit: function` AND `_git: function` (proves compsys autoloading actually finds the right files; plain `whence -w compinit` returns `none` until autoload runs, so the autoload+compinit prefix is required)

git compile-time defaults override (M4-S03 round-5 carry-forward): git binary has /data/data/com.termux/files/usr/libexec/git-core hardcoded as exec-path default; verify on device that `git --exec-path` returns /data/data/dev.warp.mobile/files/usr/libexec/git-core (set via GIT_EXEC_PATH env var at PTY spawn or via `git config --global` writing to ~/.gitconfig). Also verify `git clone https://github.com/octocat/Hello-World.git /tmp/test` succeeds (proves git-remote-https subprocess loads correctly via the patched RUNPATH + GIT_EXEC_PATH)

Broader compile-time defaults coverage (M4-S03 round-6 carry-forward — codex audit found additional categories beyond zsh+git): set the following env vars at PTY spawn in WarpTerminalService.spawnPty: TERMINFO=$PREFIX/share/terminfo (proves terminal type 'xterm-256color' resolves: `infocmp xterm-256color` returns descriptor without ENOENT), LOCPATH=$PREFIX/share/locale (proves locale data resolves: `locale -a | head` lists POSIX + at least one non-default locale). These are package-manager-toolchain-adjacent so M4-S07 may absorb them if M4-S06 scope creep is a concern.

Test driver test-pkg-install.sh GREEN on R5CX10VFFBA: 0 canonical com.termux + 18 dev.warp.mobile entries; ls --color=always produces ANSI; module_path resolves to dev path; git --exec-path retargeted. Rust pkg.rs subprocess wrapper carry-forward to M5 (per M4-go-no-go.md §4).
acceptanceCriteria: crates/warp_terminal_mobile_facade/src/pkg.rs (NEW) wraps pkg/apt subprocess
Progress channel: parse apt stdout/stderr into PkgProgress events (downloading X%, installing Y, configuring Z, done)
Cancellation: SIGTERM to pkg subprocess + cleanup partial state
Java side: PkgInstallActivity / PkgInstallDialog shows progress UI

Codex review dispatched and PASS verdict received

TLS / CA cert resolution (M4-S03 round-6+7 carry-forward — codex audit): set SSL_CERT_FILE=$PREFIX/etc/tls/cert.pem and SSL_CERT_DIR=$PREFIX/etc/tls/certs at PTY spawn (via WarpTerminalService.spawnPty env). Verify on device that `pkg install ca-certificates && update-ca-certificates` populates $PREFIX/etc/tls/certs/. Round-7 strengthened verification: `curl -vI https://github.com 2>&1 | grep -E 'CAfile:|CApath:'` shows BOTH `CAfile: $PREFIX/etc/tls/cert.pem` AND `CApath: $PREFIX/etc/tls/certs` (proves curl actually used our override, not /system/etc/security/cacerts fallback). Negative test: `env -u SSL_CERT_FILE -u SSL_CERT_DIR curl -vI https://github.com 2>&1 | grep -F com.termux` returns 1+ hits (proves Termux-compiled libcurl default points at com.termux when env not set, confirming the env override is the only safe mechanism).

apt/dpkg internal path retargeting (M4-S03 round-7 carry-forward): sources.list contains repo URLs (not $PREFIX paths) so `grep -F com.termux $PREFIX/etc/apt/sources.list` is INSUFFICIENT proof. Required device verification: `apt-config dump | grep -E '^(Dir|DPkg::Path|RootDir|Cache|State)' > /tmp/apt-conf-dump.txt` AND `grep -F com.termux /tmp/apt-conf-dump.txt | wc -l` returns `0` AND `grep -F dev.warp.mobile /tmp/apt-conf-dump.txt | wc -l` returns ≥10 (covers Dir::Bin, Dir::Cache, Dir::State, Dir::Etc::Trusted, etc.). Plus end-to-end: `apt update` exits 0 with non-empty Hit/Get lines AND `pkg install hello && hello | head -1` returns greeting (proves the full chain: apt resolves dirs → downloads → dpkg installs → binary runs).

stories.verifiedBy: Round-1 device-verified on Galaxy S24 Ultra R5CX10VFFBA. SettingsActivity (programmatic LinearLayout: EditText + Save/Test/Clear + status + cost warning) launches without crash. Auth header redacted in logs to 'Bearer sk-ant-f***...only' (last 4 chars). Java side: SettingsActivity with EditText for API key + Save + Test Connection buttons
API key persisted in Android Keystore via androidx.security.crypto.EncryptedSharedPreferences (NOT plaintext SharedPreferences)
Test Connection: POST to /v1/messages with model=claude-haiku-4-5 + max_tokens=1 + minimal prompt; verify HTTP 200 + parsed `content` field exists
Failure UX: clear error messages for 401 (invalid key), 429 (rate-limit), 500/network (transient)
Device verification: SettingsActivity launches; valid API key → Test returns OK; invalid key → 401 message; airplane-mode → network error
Codex review dispatched and PASS verdict received

stories.title: Inline ghost-text via Claude Haiku (streaming + debounce + cancel-on-keystroke)
acceptanceCriteria: crates/warp_ai_mobile/src/ghost.rs: ghost-text completion request builder + streaming response handler
Debounce: typing pauses ≥150ms before sending request; subsequent keystroke cancels in-flight request via tokio CancellationToken
Token cap: max_tokens=200 per ralplan §6 M6 #5 (ghost ≤200 p95)
Latency target: p50 first-token <500ms on flagship S24U + reasonable network (verify via timing logs)
Java side: ghost suggestion rendered as grayed text after the cursor; Tab key accepts (inserts the suggestion as if user typed); other keys reject

Device verification: type partial command 'ls -' on PTY input → ghost appears within 500ms p50 → Tab → command line becomes 'ls -la' (or whatever Haiku suggested); subsequent keystroke pre-Tab cancels cleanly without UI glitch
Codex review dispatched and PASS verdict received

stories.title: Agent task UI via Claude Sonnet (side-panel block, streaming output)
acceptanceCriteria: crates/warp_ai_mobile/src/agent.rs: agent task request builder + streaming response handler with prompt template (system: 'You are a Warp terminal agent...')
Java side: AgentBlockView (custom View added below the dynamic_grid) shows the streaming response, block-by-block
Trigger: long-press a Block + select 'Explain' from M5-S03 BottomSheet menu (or fallback: AccessoryRow 'AI' button in round-1)
Token cap: max_tokens=2000 per ralplan §6 M6 #5 (agent ≤2000 p95)
Latency target: p50 first-token <8s + complete-response p95 within 30s on S24U
Cancel: tap X on agent block cancels in-flight request
Device verification: select a real terminal block (e.g. `du -sh *` output) → tap Explain → AgentBlockView populates with explanation within 8s p50; cancel mid-stream stops cleanly
Codex review dispatched and PASS verdict received

stories.title: Offline graceful degrade + airplane-mode test
acceptanceCriteria: crates/warp_ai_mobile/src/connectivity.rs: simple ConnectivityManager wrapper exposing is_online() + onConnectivityChange callback
When offline: ghost-text + agent buttons grey out (UI signals AI unavailable); AccessoryRow + PTY + render layers continue working unchanged
When network returns: AI controls re-enable; no app restart required
Non-blocking notice: a small banner ('AI features paused — no network') shown at top of activity ONLY when AI was previously enabled and just went offline; not shown if user never set BYOK key
Device verification: airplane-mode toggle on S24U triggers the banner + grey-outs; toggle off restores within 3s
Codex review dispatched and PASS verdict received

stories.title: Token-usage display + opt-in telemetry sink
acceptanceCriteria: Settings screen shows: cumulative tokens this session (input/output split per model); cumulative tokens this billing-period (using a manual reset button — no Anthropic billing API integration in v1)
Per-request token counts appended to a local CSV log at $PREFIX/var/log/warp-ai-usage.csv: timestamp, model, kind (ghost/agent), input_tokens, output_tokens, latency_ms
Opt-in telemetry: SettingsActivity has a 'Share anonymous usage to project' switch (default OFF). When ON, post anonymized CSV row to a TBD endpoint (out-of-scope for v1; emit local file only)
Token cap warnings: ghost p95 >200 OR agent p95 >2000 over 24h logs a warning to logcat (not user-facing in v1)
Codex review dispatched and PASS verdict received

stories.title: M6 milestone close-out doc
acceptanceCriteria: .omc/m6-artifacts/M6-go-no-go.md per M5-go-no-go.md template
7-story ledger with status (PASS / PARTIAL / DEFERRED)
Ralplan §6 M6 acceptance verdict (5 ACs)
Per-layer GO/CONDITIONAL/NO-GO assessment (L0-L4 + new L5 AI)
v1-release carry-overs documented
Codex review dispatched and PASS verdict received

verifierConfig.rationale: User SOP from M0/M1/M2: every worker deliverable goes through Codex review. M2-S11 process violation lesson (worker self-PASS exposed by lead audit) makes lead-dispatched codex AFTER fix the canonical pattern for M3+. Web doc lookup precedes implementation per 「搭配 codex 和網路搜索相關文檔和資訊」.
